visa_free.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina (biometric passport holders only)
Taiwan (passport with national identity card number)
Thailand (max 60 days, max 2 entries per calendar year from Nov 2025)

visa_on_arrival.name: Indonesia (VOA or e-visa, 30 days extendable to 60)

e_visa.name: Vietnam (e-visa option for up to 90 days)

outbound_official.notes: Source is the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs 'Pas og visum til udlandet' section (um.dk), which provides individual country pages for Danish ordinary passport holders. The page confirms visa-free status, ETA requirements, visa-on-arrival, and e-visa requirements per destination. This is the authoritative origin-side Danish government source analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. Vietnam appears in both visa_free (45-day stamp-free entry) and e_visa (e-visa option for up to 90 days). Biometric passport conditions apply to several Balkan/Eastern European countries. China visa-free arrangement is temporary (valid until 31 Dec 2026 per kina.um.dk). All EU/EEA/Schengen countries are visa-free under freedom of movement. The list covers major destinations verified as of June 2026; um.dk has individual pages for ~195 countries.

visa_policy.notes: Denmark is a Schengen Area member. Visa-free travelers are subject to the standard Schengen 90-days-in-180-day-period rule unless special Nordic bilateral rules apply (noted per nationality). Countries listed on the official visa requirement page that hold biometric passports may be exempt from visa requirement as noted. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is not yet operational as of June 2026; expected in last quarter of 2026; fee set at EUR 20. Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR, and British National Overseas (BNO) passport holders are visa-exempt under ordinary passport rules and are listed separately in visa_policy.

conditional_access.conditions: Aliens with a valid residence permit for another Schengen State are exempt under Article 21 of the Schengen Convention from the requirement of a visa for Denmark for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Time spent on a residence permit in Denmark or another Schengen country is not counted toward the 90-day limit. Residence permits valid only for the Faroe Islands or Greenland are not valid for other Schengen entries.

credential.subtype: Type D (long-stay visa / national visa, valid more than 90 days)

conditional_access.conditions: Aliens with a visa valid for more than 90 days for a specific Schengen country (long-term visa / D visa) are exempt under Article 21 of the Schengen Convention from the requirement of a visa for Denmark for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

credential.subtype: re-entry permit (listed in Annex 2 to the Visa Code Handbook)

conditional_access.conditions: In certain cases, aliens with a re-entry permit issued by another Schengen State are also exempt from the visa requirement, provided the re-entry permit is listed in Annex 2 to the Visa Code Handbook.

credential.subtype: EU residence card (Directive 2004/38/EC, Article 10 or Article 20)

conditional_access.conditions: Aliens in possession of an EU residence card issued under Article 10 or Article 20 of Directive 2004/38/EC are exempt under Article 5 of the Directive from any visa requirement to enter and stay in Denmark. This applies to family members of EU/EEA/Swiss citizens exercising free movement rights.
Only holders of biometric diplomatic passports from Albania are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Only holders of diplomatic passports from Armenia are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Citizens of Bolivia with diplomatic and service passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Only holders of diplomatic passports from Bosnia and Herzegovina are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.

Only holders of diplomatic passports from China (People's Republic of China) are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties. Note: holders of Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR passports are visa-free under ordinary passport rules (listed separately in visa_policy).
Citizens of Egypt with diplomatic and service passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Citizens of India with diplomatic passports, service passports, and official passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Only holders of biometric diplomatic and biometric service passports from Indonesia are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Only holders of diplomatic passports from Kazakhstan are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.

Only holders of diplomatic passports from Moldova are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties. Note: biometric ordinary passport holders are already visa-free under ordinary passport rules.

Only holders of diplomatic passports from Montenegro are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties. Note: biometric ordinary passport holders are already visa-free under ordinary passport rules.
Citizens of Morocco with diplomatic and service passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Citizens of Pakistan with diplomatic passports, service passports, and official passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Citizens of the Philippines with diplomatic and service passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.

Only holders of diplomatic passports from Serbia are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties. Note: biometric ordinary passport holders are already visa-free under ordinary passport rules.
Citizens of Thailand with diplomatic passports, service passports, and official passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Only holders of diplomatic passports from Tunisia are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.
Citizens of Turkey (Turkiye) with diplomatic and service passports are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties.

Only holders of diplomatic passports and biometric service passports from Ukraine are exempt from the visa requirement, irrespective of whether they enter Denmark to perform official duties. Note: biometric ordinary passport holders are already visa-free under ordinary passport rules.

cbi.notes: Denmark has no citizenship-by-investment program. Citizenship is acquired through naturalization requiring 9 years of continuous legal residence (reduced periods apply for refugees, Nordic citizens, and spouses). Denmark has allowed dual/multiple citizenship since September 1, 2015. Source: https://lifeindenmark.borger.dk/settle-in-denmark/danish-citizenship/conditions-for-foreign-citizens--acquisition-of-danish-citizenship

programs.name: Pay Limit Scheme
programs.notes: Requires job offer with annual salary of DKK 552,000 or more (2026 level, updated 1 January each year). Not an investment-based program; it is a salary-threshold work permit. Minimum 30 hours/week. Permanent residence requires 8 years legal residence (or 4 years meeting all supplementary requirements). Citizenship requires 9 years residence. Processing fee DKK 6,810 (2026 rate confirmed).

programs.name: Start-up Denmark
programs.notes: Entrepreneurial residence permit for innovative startup founders. Business idea must be approved by Danish Business Authority expert panel. Minimum personal funds DKK 153,240 for single applicant (2025 level - 2026 figure not yet confirmed on official page as of June 2026). Maximum 75 permits per year. Initial permit valid up to 2 years, renewable for up to 3 years at a time. Processing time: 1 month. Fee: DKK 3,060 (subject to annual update per SIRI fee notice January 2026).

programs.name: Fast-Track Scheme
programs.eligibility: Must be employed by a SIRI-certified company; job must meet salary and standards of one of five tracks (Pay Limit, Supplementary Pay Limit, Short-Term, Researcher, Educational)
programs.processing_time: 1 month standard; up to 3 months for supplementary track with additional info required
programs.notes: Five tracks: (1) Pay Limit - DKK 552,000/year minimum salary (2026 confirmed); (2) Supplementary Pay Limit - DKK 446,000/year minimum (2026 confirmed); (3) Short-Term - max 90 days in 365-day rolling period; (4) Researcher - must have specific research-related tie to the individual; (5) Educational - for highly qualified training. Processing fee DKK 6,810 (2026 rate confirmed). Employer must be SIRI-certified. Updated income statistics applicable from 1 April 2026 per SIRI notice.

programs.name: Positive List for People with Higher Education
programs.eligibility: Must have job offer in a profession on the official Positive List (updated 1 January and 1 July each year); salary and employment terms must meet Danish standards
programs.notes: Updated twice yearly (1 January and 1 July). As of 1 January 2026, the Positive List for Higher Education includes 183 job titles; the Positive List for Skilled Work includes 57 job titles. Processing fee DKK 6,810. No employer certification required (unlike Fast-Track). Managed by SIRI.

programs.name: Positive List for Skilled Work
programs.eligibility: Must have job offer in a skilled profession on the official Positive List; salary must correspond to Danish professional standards; employer must meet apprenticeship obligations
programs.notes: Updated twice yearly (1 January and 1 July). As of 1 January 2026, includes 57 skilled job titles experiencing labor shortages. Employer must demonstrate meeting apprenticeship targets. Salary paid to Danish bank account required. Processing fee DKK 6,810.

programs.name: Start-up Denmark
programs.eligibility: Foreign entrepreneur with innovative business idea approved by Danish Business Authority expert panel; must be full or partial owner and actively participate in management; teams of up to 3 people may apply together
programs.notes: Maximum 75 permits per year. Business cannot be routine trade (restaurant, retail, import/export without innovation). Must demonstrate sufficient funds for first year (DKK 153,240 at 2025 level for single person without family). Initial permit valid up to 2 years; renewable for up to 3 years at a time. Processing fee DKK 3,060 (subject to annual update per SIRI fee notice January 2026).

CONFIRMED: Fast-Track Scheme salary thresholds DKK 552,000 (Pay Limit track) and DKK 446,000 (Supplementary track) for 2026 - verified against official page.
CONFIRMED: Processing fee DKK 6,810 for Fast-Track and Positive List schemes - verified for 2026.

CONFIRMED: No CBI program in Denmark - verified.
CONFIRMED: No golden visa / investor visa - verified.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Visum/Countries-with-a-visa-requirement-and-visa-free-countries - full visa-free list re-confirmed against official page. All entries in visa_policy.visa_free match the live official page as of 2026-06-27. No new countries added to or removed from the visa-free list since 2026-06-14.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Visa-free-visits - special Nordic entry rules for Australia, Canada, Chile, Israel, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, South Korea, and USA re-confirmed unchanged.

2026-06-27 CONFIRMED: Turkey does NOT appear in the official nyidanmark.dk visa-free list for ordinary passport holders. Turkey appears in the visa-REQUIRED list with *** annotation (service providers under EC-Turkey Association Agreement have a separate exemption as noted in conditional_access and gaps). Turkey is not in visa_policy.visa_free - confirmed correct.
2026-06-27 CONFIRMED: South Africa is visa-REQUIRED for ordinary passport holders entering Denmark. South Africa correctly absent from visa_policy.visa_free.
2026-06-27 CONFIRMED: Morocco is visa-REQUIRED for ordinary passport holders entering Denmark (appears with * annotation in visa-required list). Morocco correctly absent from visa_policy.visa_free.

2026-06-27 CONFIRMED: ETIAS remains not yet operational as of June 2026. Fee is EUR 20. Expected launch Q4 2026 (approx. 6 months after EES which started 12 October 2025). No mandatory ETIAS in effect as of this verification date.
2026-06-27 CONFIRMED: No new Denmark-specific bilateral visa-free agreements found for 2026. No new countries added to Schengen Annex II list affecting Denmark in 2026.

2026-06-27 NOTE: Georgia, Kosovo, North Macedonia appear in both the visa-free and visa-required lists on nyidanmark.dk - the visa-free entries are conditional on biometric passports. Current record correctly captures this with biometric-condition notes in visa_policy.visa_free.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Visum/Countries-with-a-visa-requirement-and-visa-free-countries - full visa-free list re-confirmed. Page last updated 24-07-2025. No new countries added to or removed from the visa-free list since 2026-06-27 re-verification. Vanuatu confirmed in visa-REQUIRED section only (visa requirement reintroduced via EU Regulation 2025/11, effective 3 February 2025).

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR, and British National Overseas (BNO) passport holders confirmed visa-free for Denmark on the official nyidanmark.dk country list page: 'citizens with a Hong Kong Special Administrative Region passport, a Región Administrativa Especial de Macau passport or a British National Overseas passport are exempt from the visa requirement.' All three entries in visa_policy.visa_free are confirmed correct.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Pay Limit Scheme minimum DKK 552,000 for 2026 re-confirmed on official page. Supplementary Pay Limit DKK 446,000 for 2026 confirmed from search results. All fee amounts (DKK 6,810 for Fast-Track/Pay Limit/Positive List; DKK 3,060 for Start-up Denmark; DKK 8,490 for Family Reunification) confirmed from official fee overview page.

2026-06-29 CORRECTED: Student Residence Permit fee updated from null to DKK 3,060 (~USD 450). Previously listed as 'not confirmed'; now confirmed from the official SIRI fee overview page (nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/Faelles/Fees/Fee---overview-of-fee-rates, verified 2026-06-29). Notes in visa_types student entry updated accordingly.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Vanuatu not in visa_policy.visa_free - correct. EU Regulation 2025/11 (adopted 19 December 2024) moved Vanuatu from Schengen Annex II (visa-free) to Annex I (visa-required), effective 3 February 2025. Reason: Vanuatu investor citizenship programs posed security concerns. Record correctly has Vanuatu absent from visa_policy.visa_free.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Start-up Denmark minimum funds DKK 153,240 still labeled '2025 level' on official page (page last updated July 2025); no updated 2026 figure published on the page. Gap preserved.
gaps: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) expected in last quarter of 2026 but not yet operational. When operational, visa-free travelers will need ETIAS travel authorization (EUR 20 fee, valid 3 years). Record will need updating when ETIAS launches.

Start-up Denmark minimum self-support funds for 2026 (as opposed to 2025 level DKK 153,240) not confirmed on official page as of June 2026 re-verification; official page still cites '2025 level' figure. SIRI fee rates news item (January 2026) exists but specific self-support fund update for 2026 not accessible from the Start-up Denmark page.
No digital nomad visa found on official Danish government sources.
No golden visa / investor visa program found on official Danish sources; Denmark does not offer a residence-by-investment (golden visa) scheme.
Permanent residence path_to_pr_years and path_to_citizenship_years in rbi programs reflect general Danish naturalization rules, not program-specific accelerated timelines.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: No official Danish government source found granting conditional entry to Denmark based on OCI cardholder status. Not recorded.

Credential-based entry for holders of US, UK, Canadian, Australian, or Japanese visas (distinct from Schengen/EU credentials): No official Danish source found granting general visa-free entry to Denmark based solely on holding such third-country visas. These are only mentioned in the context of airport transit visa exemptions, not full entry.
Turkish service-provider entry under EC-Turkey Association Agreement (Section IV.27 of Overview PDF): nationality-based rule, not credential-based, so not included in conditional_access.

The PDF overview (Overview-of-travel-documents-visa-requirements-and-border-crossing-points180625.pdf) remains inaccessible for full text extraction (binary format, unreadable by WebFetch). Some conditional_access details are based on the previously verified June 2025 version of this document. If a newer version of this PDF is published (filename date prefix would change from '180625'), it should be re-checked.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) – Tourism/Visit
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals from visa-required countries to visit Denmark (and the wider Schengen Area) for tourism, visiting family or friends, or other private purposes.
visa_types.notes: Standard Schengen Type C visa valid for the entire Schengen zone. Maximum stay 90 days in any 180-day period. Fee is the standard EU Schengen fee (EUR 80, ~USD 90). Applications are submitted at the Danish embassy or consulate (or VFS/BLS application centre) in the applicant's country of residence. Entry and exit days both count toward the 90-day limit. Applicant must show financial means of approximately DKK 350/day (private accommodation) or DKK 500/day (hotel).

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) – Business
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals from visa-required countries to visit Denmark for business meetings, negotiations, conferences, or other commercial activities for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Same Schengen Type C visa as for tourism but with business purpose stated in application. Does not permit paid employment in Denmark. Maximum 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period. Standard EU Schengen fee (EUR 80, ~USD 90). Applied for at the Danish embassy or consulate or authorised VFS/BLS centre in the applicant's country of residence.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (ATV)
visa_types.purpose: Required for certain nationalities to transit through Danish international airports (international zone) without entering Denmark.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of countries listed on Annex I of the EU Visa Code who are transiting at Copenhagen Airport (CPH) or other Danish airports. Exempt if holding a valid Schengen/EU visa, US/UK/Canadian/Japanese/Australian visa or residence permit, as listed in Annex II. Standard Schengen ATV fee (EUR 80, ~USD 90). Applied for at Danish embassy or consulate.

visa_types.name: Pay Limit Scheme (Løngrænseordningen)
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals who have received a job offer in Denmark with a salary at or above the annual threshold (DKK 552,000 in 2026), regardless of occupation or education level.
visa_types.notes: Fee is DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Minimum salary DKK 552,000/year (2026, adjusted annually 1 January). Salary must be paid into a Danish bank account in the applicant's name within 180 days. Minimum 30 hours/week. Permit is typically 4 years for employment of 4+ years, or for the employment period plus a 6-month job-seeking extension for shorter contracts. Applicant must not receive social benefits. Family members (spouse/partner and children under 18) may accompany. Applications submitted online via nyidanmark.dk.

visa_types.name: Fast-Track Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Accelerated residence and work permit for employees of SIRI-certified companies, offering five tracks: Pay Limit, Supplementary Pay Limit, Short-Term, Researcher, and Educational.
visa_types.notes: Fee is DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Requires employer to be SIRI-certified. Five tracks: (1) Pay Limit – DKK 552,000/year minimum; (2) Supplementary Pay Limit – DKK 446,000/year minimum (requires Jobnet/EURES posting for 2 weeks); (3) Short-Term – max 90 days in 365 days, non-extendable; (4) Researcher – specific research role; (5) Educational – highly qualified training. Standard processing 1 month; Quick Job Start allows work start within 0–30 days if applicant can enter Denmark legally. Employer applies online.

visa_types.name: Positive List for People with Higher Education
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals who have a job offer in a profession listed on the Danish Positive List for Higher Education (shortage occupations updated twice yearly).
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Positive List updated 1 January and 1 July each year; as of 1 January 2026 includes 183 job titles. Salary must meet Danish professional standards; must be paid to a Danish bank account. No employer certification required. Processing time approximately 1 month. Applications submitted online via nyidanmark.dk.

visa_types.name: Positive List for Skilled Work
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals who have a job offer in a skilled trade or craft occupation on the Danish Positive List for Skilled Work (shortage occupations updated twice yearly).
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Positive List updated twice yearly; as of 1 January 2026 includes 57 skilled job titles. Employer must demonstrate meeting apprenticeship training obligations. Salary paid to Danish bank account required. Processing time approximately 1 month.

visa_types.name: Researcher Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals employed in a paid research position at a Danish research institution or company, where the role has specific research-related ties to the individual's qualifications.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Salary must meet Danish professional standards; only liquid salary and pension contributions count. Primary purpose must be research. Permit duration matches employment contract (up to 4 years). Biometrics required within 14 days of application. Permit holder may change positions within same employer, take sideline employment, and serve as external examiner at other universities without additional permits. Family members (spouse/partner and children under 18) may accompany.

visa_types.name: Guest Researcher Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit for non-EU/EEA academics with a master's degree who are invited to conduct research at a Danish institution but are not employed (self-funded or externally funded).
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Applicant must hold at minimum a master's degree and have a written invitation from a Danish research institution providing facilities without employment. Must demonstrate financial self-sufficiency (DKK 12,770/month for single applicant in 2025). Maximum permit duration 3 years; expires 14 days after invitation lapses. Permit holder is exempt from work permit requirement for sideline employment. Family members may accompany.

visa_types.name: Trainee Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals employed as trainees in Denmark as part of an educational course connected to their home-country employer, under a verified strategic cooperation agreement.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Maximum stay 24 months total; individual permits up to 1 year. Requires verified strategic cooperation or partnership between a foreign company and the Danish employer. Must follow an educational course relevant to the job the applicant will return to abroad. Permit is employer-specific; changing positions within the same employer requires new application. Family members may accompany.

visa_types.name: Special Individual Qualifications (Athletes, Coaches, and Other Unique Roles)
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA nationals who possess unique, individually assessed qualifications for a specific role where no other person can immediately take on the job - most commonly used for professional athletes, jockeys, and coaches.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 6,810 (~USD 1,001). Permit validity is tiered: up to 1 year initially, up to 2 years in years 2–4, up to 3 years after 4 years of stays. Applicant can only work at the specified employer; new employment requires new application (may begin work upon submission). Salary must meet Danish professional standards. Family members may accompany.

visa_types.name: Sideline Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA nationals already holding a Danish residence and work permit to take additional part-time employment that is naturally related to their primary job, without requiring a new full residence permit.
visa_types.notes: No fee. Work-only permit; cannot serve as basis for residence. Typically 8–15 hours per week. The sideline work must be naturally related to the primary permit-basis job. Researchers, guest researchers, PhD students, artists, and professional athletes automatically have unlimited sideline employment rights without applying.

visa_types.name: Start-up Denmark
visa_types.purpose: Residence and work permit for non-EU/EEA entrepreneurs with an innovative business idea approved by the Danish Business Authority expert panel who wish to establish and operate a new company in Denmark.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 3,060 (~USD 450). Business idea must be approved by Danish Business Authority before applying to SIRI. Maximum 75 permits issued per year. Teams of up to 3 persons may apply together. Minimum personal funds DKK 153,240 (2025 level; single applicant). Initial permit up to 2 years; renewable for up to 3 years at a time. Applicant must be full or partial owner and actively involved in management. Permit is company-specific.

visa_types.name: Student Residence Permit (Higher Education)
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit for non-EU/EEA nationals enrolled as students at a Danish university or accredited higher education institution.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 3,060 (~USD 450) confirmed from official fee overview page (nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/Faelles/Fees/Fee---overview-of-fee-rates, verified 2026-06-29). Permit duration matches the standard (nominal) duration of the study programme. Students may work up to 20 hours per week during the academic year (September–May) and full-time in June–August. Applicants must be enrolled at an approved Danish educational institution. Applications submitted online via nyidanmark.dk.

visa_types.name: PhD Student Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit for non-EU/EEA nationals enrolled as PhD students at a Danish university, whether the position is salaried or unpaid.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 3,060 (~USD 450). Valid for the standard duration of the PhD programme. Salaried PhD students are self-supporting; unpaid students must demonstrate DKK 7,426/month (2026 level). Work rights: 90 hours/month during academic months, full-time in June–August, unlimited for work naturally related to PhD. Post-graduation: 3-year job-seeking period with same work rights. EU/EEA/Swiss nationals apply for an EU residence document instead.

visa_types.name: Family Reunification – Spouse or Cohabiting Partner
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit allowing the non-EU/EEA spouse or registered/cohabiting partner of a person residing legally in Denmark to join and live with that person.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 8,490 (~USD 1,248). Both applicant and sponsor must be at least 24 years old (exceptions for children or serious illness). Relationship must be legally valid and voluntary. Applicant and sponsor together must meet 4 of 6 integration conditions. Sponsor must have independent housing of at least 20 sq. metres per occupant, be self-supporting, and post a financial guarantee of DKK 61,709.34 (2026). Sponsor must also have passed 'Prøve i Dansk 3' or equivalent. Processing time up to 10 months. Initial permit typically 2 years; renewable.

visa_types.name: Accompanying Family Member Permit (Work/Study-based)
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit allowing non-EU/EEA spouses, registered partners, cohabiting partners, and children under 18 to accompany a primary permit holder who is living in Denmark for work or study.
visa_types.notes: Fee DKK 3,080 (~USD 453). Permit duration matches the sponsor's permit. Holder is generally entitled to work in Denmark without a separate work permit (exception: employment at the sponsor's own company or closely related entities requires a separate application). Cohabiting partners must demonstrate at least 1.5–2 years of cohabitation. Family members must reside at the same address as the sponsor. Cannot receive public social benefits.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Entry (Passport-Based Exemption)
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry to Denmark for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports from countries that have bilateral visa-exemption agreements with Denmark, allowing official and non-official visits without a Schengen visa.
visa_types.notes: No visa required. Applies to holders of diplomatic/service/official passports from countries with bilateral agreements with Denmark, including (but not limited to): Bolivia (diplomatic + service), China (diplomatic only), Egypt (diplomatic + service), India (diplomatic + service + official), Indonesia (biometric diplomatic + biometric service), Morocco (diplomatic + service), Pakistan (diplomatic + service + official), Philippines (diplomatic + service), Thailand (diplomatic + service + official), Turkey (diplomatic + service). Albania, Armenia, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Kazakhstan, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine (diplomatic only for most). Full list in the official PDF overview document.

visa_types.name: Visa-Free Short Stay (Schengen 90/180 Rule)
visa_types.purpose: Allows nationals of visa-exempt countries to enter and stay in Denmark (and the Schengen Area) for up to 90 days in any 180-day period for tourism, business, family visits, or other short-term purposes without obtaining a visa.
visa_types.notes: No visa required. Valid travel document must be valid for at least 3 months beyond intended departure and issued within the last 10 years. Must show sufficient financial means (approx. DKK 350/day private or DKK 500/day hotel), documentation of visit purpose, and must not be registered in the Schengen Information System or listed on UN/EU sanctions. Special bilateral arrangements for nationals of Australia, Canada, Chile, Israel, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, South Korea, and the USA allow 90 days/3 months calculated from first Nordic entry. ETIAS authorization will be required once launched (expected Q4 2026; EUR 20 fee).
